verdict_reasoning: B2M is the invariant light chain of MHC class I (and MHC-I-like molecules: CD1, HFE, FcRn, MR1). It has a signal peptide, no TM domain of its own, but assembles intracellularly with the MHC-I heavy chain and is co-trafficked to the cell surface as an obligate, stable non-covalent partner. It is broadly displayed on virtually all nucleated cells as part of surface MHC-I, accessible to antibodies on intact cells (clinical anti-B2M antibodies and surface flow cytometry routinely detect it). Soluble shed B2M exists in serum, but the canonical biological form is surface-anchored via the MHC-I heavy chain.
